message:
fix(storage): skip directories in backup cleanup, tighten retention assertion, add directory-ignoring test

The cleanup_old_backups function previously treated any filesystem entry
matching the backup prefix as a backup candidate, including directories
that happened to have backup-like names. This could cause incorrect
retention counting (directory counted toward keep_count) and potential
errors when attempting to delete a directory with fs::remove_file.

Changes:
- Added meta.is_file() guard to the backup enumeration chain, ensuring
  only regular files are counted toward retention and selected for
  deletion. Directories with backup-like names are silently skipped.
- Tightened the existing retention test assertion from assert!(len <= 3)
  to assert_eq!(len, 3) since the test creates exactly 5 backups with
  keep_count=3, so exactly 3 should remain.
- Added test cleanup_old_backups_ignores_backup_named_directories that
  creates 3 backup files plus a directory named test.db.backup.directory,
  calls cleanup with keep_count=2, and verifies that only 2 files remain
  while the directory is untouched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
